🎯 Ideal Customer Profile

Change Risk Radar is purpose-built for companies where a surprise vendor change could cause an outage, a compliance gap, or a billing shock — before they can react.

✅ Strong fit
  • ✓B2B SaaS with $1M–$50M ARR
  • ✓Uses Stripe for billing (any MRR)
  • ✓AWS CloudTrail or Google Workspace in production
  • ✓Engineering or DevOps team ≥ 3 people
  • ✓Had a vendor-change incident in last 12 months
  • ✓CTO, VP Eng, or Head of Ops as buyer
  • ✓SOC 2, ISO 27001, or HIPAA compliance motion
  • ✓Shopify Plus merchant or Salesforce org ≥ 50 seats
⚠️ Weaker fit
  • ✗Pre-revenue or pre-product company
  • ✗Single-vendor stack (nothing to cross-correlate)
  • ✗No technical buyer in evaluation
  • ✗Consumer app without vendor dependencies
  • ✗Agencies or consultancies (no own stack to monitor)
  • ✗Requires on-prem deployment (we're cloud-native)
B2B SaaS
Stripe for billing + Workspace or AWS
Common trigger: Pricing incident, API deprecation, security review
E-Commerce
Shopify Plus + Stripe + AWS infra
Common trigger: Payment terms change, shipping SLA shift, app fee increase
DevTools / Infra
AWS-heavy + GitHub + Salesforce org
Common trigger: IAM policy change, API quota reduction, critical update

☑️ Qualification Checklist

Use these questions in a 20-minute discovery call to assess fit and urgency. A "yes" on 4+ makes them a hot prospect.

PainQ1. Has a vendor change ever caused an outage, billing surprise, or compliance gap you found out about late?
Why it matters: Establishes historical pain. If yes: ask for the story — it becomes your ROI anchor.
🔴 High
PainQ2. How do you currently find out when Stripe, AWS, or Workspace makes a change that affects you?
Why it matters: Most honest answers: 'email newsletters', 'Twitter', or 'when something breaks'. All are buying signals.
🔴 High
FitQ3. Which of these do you use in production: Stripe, AWS CloudTrail, Google Workspace, Shopify, Salesforce?
Why it matters: 2+ = strong connector coverage day one. 0 = observatory mode only, weaker value prop.
🔴 High
FitQ4. How many engineers or operations people would receive and act on these alerts?
Why it matters: 1 → solo champion risk. 3+ → multi-threaded deal, lower churn risk.
🟡 Medium
TimelineQ5. Are you actively evaluating vendor monitoring tools now, or is this exploratory?
Why it matters: Active eval → 30-day pilot window. Exploratory → nurture, invite to demo.
🟡 Medium
AuthorityQ6. Who else needs to sign off on a $500–1,500/month security or ops tool?
Why it matters: Identifies real buyer. Engineering-led purchases close faster. Finance-gated → longer cycle.
🔴 High
BudgetQ7. Do you have budget this quarter for a security/operations monitoring tool in the $6k–18k/year range?
Why it matters: Direct budget question. If budget cycle is future → set follow-up date.
🟡 Medium
UrgencyQ8. Do you have a security review, SOC 2 audit, or compliance deadline coming up in the next 90 days?
Why it matters: Deadline = urgency = faster close. SOC 2 / ISO 27001 buyers move quickly when audit is imminent.
🔴 High
Scoring: 5–6 High-weight "yes" → Hot (send Pilot SOW same day) · 3–4 → Warm (start 14-day trial, follow up at day 7) · 1–2 → Cold (add to newsletter, invite to watch demo)

🥊 Objection Handling

Common objections and proven responses. Be direct — don't over-engineer the answer.

Awareness
"We already get emails from Stripe / AWS / Google."
Those are marketing or engineering updates — not risk-assessed for your specific stack. Change Risk Radar tells you which changes affect your integration specifically, with the impact to your business and the exact action to take. Email newsletters don't do that.
DIY
"We have engineers who track this manually."
How many hours a week? At $150/hr for a senior engineer, that's $6k/month in attention tax — more than our Growth plan. And engineers catch things reactively, not proactively. We catch changes before they hit production.
Price
"$500/month is too expensive."
One missed Stripe payment-term change costs more than a year of Starter. Walk me through your last vendor-change incident — what did it cost in eng time and customer impact? I bet it was more than $6k.
Compliance
"Can you handle our compliance requirements?"
We have a security pack and DPA you can download right now — no sales call required. RLS on every row, SOC 2-ready audit logs, least-privilege connector scopes, GDPR-compliant. [Link: /pilot/security and /legal/dpa]
Security review
"We need to talk to our security team first."
Perfect — here's the security pack they'll want to review: infrastructure, data classification, connector permissions, and our sub-processor list. I'll send the DPA too. What's the best email for them?
Access concern
"We're worried about giving you API access."
Every connector is read-only. Stripe uses a Restricted Key with 8 resources and zero write access. AWS uses a cross-account IAM role with CloudTrail_ReadOnlyAccess only. Google Workspace needs 2 OAuth scopes — both read-only. Your team can audit the exact permissions in our security pack.
Roadmap
"We need a custom integration for [X]."
We're adding new connectors monthly. What's [X]? If it's Stripe, AWS, Workspace, Shopify, or Salesforce, you're covered today. If it's something else, our Custom URL monitor covers any public-facing doc — and API connectors are on our roadmap. What's your target vendor?
Timing
"We want to wait until after our next funding round / audit."
That's exactly when you need this most. Investors and auditors want to see that you have a vendor change monitoring process. We can set up a 90-day pilot that runs parallel to your audit — our pilot SOW is downloadable now, no negotiation needed.

A fully interactive demo with synthetic data — no credentials, no setup. Shows exactly what a customer sees when a critical vendor change hits.
